title: Listening Covenant
description: The parsed form of a listening covenant (akouo/v0.7): a small, human-written declaration of sonic sovereignty that a listening system carries with it. Rules the engine can execute are enforced; every line it cannot execute is kept verbatim as a commitment — the covenant is a bridge language between a community's protocol and a machine's behavior, not a guarantee of obedience. Covenants declare their lineage (e.g. the Algophonya manifesto) through `extends`.

id.description: Stable slug for this covenant, optionally with a version: 'river-covenant' or 'river-covenant/2'.

name.description: Human name, usually the document's first heading.

contract.description: The akouo contract this covenant was parsed under, e.g. 'akouo/v0.7'.

extends.description: Lineage: manifestos, community protocols, or parent covenants this one stands on (e.g. 'algophonya/v7').

rules.description: The executable subset: rules a conforming engine enforces at its input, content, output, and retention gates.

commitments.description: Lines the engine cannot (or does not yet) execute, carried verbatim and reported alongside every hearing made under the covenant. Commitments are addressed to humans and to future machines; dropping them is a conformance violation even though executing them is not required.

because.description: The covenant's reasons, verbatim — the manifesto layer of the document.

source_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the covenant's source text, so records can carry the covenant's identity without carrying its content.

source_text.description: The original document. Optional: hosts may keep the text local and share only identity + hash.

verb.description: do_not_listen: refuse a source before capture. ignore: release a content class after triage — heard, then let go, not analyzed, not stored. do_not_reveal: withhold an aspect from every output (transcript, speaker-identity, affect, location, song-identity, events, spectral-detail, or a claim category). do_not_retain: forbid retention (raw-audio, memory, location). coarsen: degrade precision instead of withholding (location to N km, time to N minutes). quiet_hours: a daily window in which the ear refuses to open. max_window: cap the seconds any single capture may hold. require_consent: refuse an operation unless the host presents an explicit consent marker.

subject.description: What the verb governs: a source (microphone, system-output, file, remote), a content class (speech, music), an aspect (transcript, speaker-identity, affect, location, song-identity, events, spectral-detail), or a retention target (raw-audio, memory, location).

args.description: Verb-specific parameters: {km} for coarsen location, {start, end} for quiet_hours, {seconds} for max_window.

text.description: The covenant line exactly as its author wrote it.
